mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a contraction in supply that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or HOUST does not decline by at least 10% over the 2-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or UNRATE does not rise by at least 1% over the 1-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month decline in housing starts signals a contraction in supply that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or both INDPRO and HOUST do not decline by at least 5% and 10% over the 2-month and 1-month periods, respectively.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or both UNRATE and PAYEMS do not rise by at least 1% and decline by at least 5% over the 2-month and 1-month periods, respectively.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 1-month decline in the CPI signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
